prompts.prompt: can u rite a python funtion solve(n) that retuns the sum of all even numbrs from 1 to n inclusive
write a function solve(s) that revereses a string and makes the whole thing uppercase plz
i need a function solve(nums) that returns the length of the longest strictly increasing subsequence in a list of ints. e.g [10,9,2,5,3,7,101,18] shud give 4
write solve(s) that returns True if the parantheses in the string are balanced (handles ()[]{}) else False. so '([]{})' is true and '([)]' is false
whats the derivative of x^3 evaluated at x=2? i just need the numbr
the integral of 2x from 0 to 3, what is it equal to
help me rite an email to my professer askin for an extension on my essay cuz i was sick this week, its due tmrw and i dont wanna fail
prompts.rubric: Professional but warm tone, clearly states the reason, makes a specific ask with a proposed new date, concise, no grammar errors.

prompts.prompt: write a short email to my landlord that the heating has been broken for 3 days now and i want it fixd asap but keep it profesional
prompts.rubric: Polite but firm, states the issue and duration, requests timely repair, professional tone, concise.

prompts.prompt: i got two job offers, one pays more but has way longer hours, the other is more chill but less money. what should i be thinkin about here
prompts.rubric: Balanced, surfaces concrete trade-offs (finances, growth, burnout, life stage), asks clarifying considerations, actionable, not preachy.

prompts.prompt: write a short paragraph arguing why public libraries are still importent in the digital age
prompts.rubric: Clear thesis, 2-3 concrete supporting points, persuasive, well-structured, no grammar errors.

prompts.prompt: prove that the simple symmetric random walk in 3 dimensions is not recurrent (i.e. it is transient). keep it under 300 words
prompts.rubric: A valid proof shows the expected number of returns to the origin is finite (equivalently the return probability is < 1), typically via the local CLT estimate P(S_2n = 0) ~ c n^{-3/2}, whose sum over n converges in 3D. Hand-waving 'it spreads out so it won't come back' without the convergence argument is invalid.

prompts.prompt: given that p implies q, and that (not p) implies r, and that q is false, show that r must be true
prompts.rubric: Valid reasoning: from q false and (p -> q), modus tollens gives not p; from not p and (not p -> r), modus ponens gives r. Must actually invoke modus tollens then modus ponens, not just assert the conclusion.
